observations.value: POST /session with any credentials (e.g. identifier=baduser, secret=badpass) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a welcome page — authentication is not validated; any username/password combination succeeds.
POST /session always sets the same static cookie value 'session=ok' regardless of user identity, meaning all sessions are indistinguishable and there is no real session management.

POST /order with a valid card (e.g. card=4111111111111111) always returns HTTP 200 with order confirmation 'Order #A1024 confirmed.' — the order number is hardcoded and never increments across repeated calls, indicating a broken/stubbed order-creation flow.

POST /cart/checkout (the 'Proceed to checkout' action from the cart page) reaches the Payment page (HTTP 200) without requiring any session cookie — unauthenticated users can proceed directly to payment without being redirected to login.
